morphology.summary: In English, algorithm is not synchronically transparent; its form preserves a historical personal-name pathway.

etymology.summary: The word traces through algorism/algorithm forms associated with the name of al-Khwarizmi.

semantic_drift.summary: The sense shifts from arithmetic methods associated with decimal calculation to any precise computational procedure.

developments.description: A name-linked arithmetic tradition becomes a term for calculation procedures.
The concept generalizes across mathematics, computing, and automated decision systems.

present_usage.summary: Modern use centers on step-by-step procedures, especially in mathematics and computing.

senses.definition: A finite or specified procedure for solving a problem or performing a computation.

examples.text: The search algorithm ranked documents by several signals.
examples.note: Original EI usage example for this capsule.

senses.definition: An automated ranking or decision mechanism treated as an opaque institutional force.

examples.text: Creators complained that the algorithm had stopped showing their work.
examples.note: Original EI usage example for this capsule.

cultural_frame.summary: The word carries frames of mathematical rigor and opaque platform power.

frames.label: formal procedure
frames.description: Frames action as rule-governed and reproducible.

frames.label: platform authority
frames.description: Frames automated systems as hidden arbiters of visibility or choice.

pragmatics.summary: Algorithm can imply precision, neutrality, opacity, or institutional blame.

stances.label: opaque authority
stances.description: Invokes hidden automated decisions affecting people.

expansion.paragraph: Algorithm preserves a historical path from a mathematician-associated name into a general term for formal procedures. In technical contexts it means a defined method for computation or problem-solving. In contemporary platform discourse, it can also name an opaque automated authority that ranks, recommends, or decides.

trace.contribution: The word traces through algorism/algorithm forms associated with the name of al-Khwarizmi.
The sense shifts from arithmetic methods associated with decimal calculation to any precise computational procedure.
Algorithm can imply precision, neutrality, opacity, or institutional blame.

capsule_summary: A name-derived computational term whose modern meanings range from formal procedure to opaque platform mechanism.

unresolved_questions: Add direct scholarly citation for the al-Khwarizmi transmission path.
